# MLE & MAP

## Subtopics
- Likelihood `L(θ) = P(D|θ)` (function of model params)
- MLE `θ_MLE = argmax_θ P(D|θ)`
- Log-likelihood `ℓ(θ) = log L(θ) = Σ log P(xᵢ|θ)` (because `log(ab)=log a + log b`)
- Common MLE results: categorical `θ̂_k = n_k/n`; Gaussian `μ̂ = (1/N)Σxᵢ`
- Posterior & MAP `P(θ|D) = P(D|θ)P(θ)/P(D)`; `θ_MAP = argmax_θ P(D|θ)P(θ)`

## Derivations to be able to reproduce
- Derive `θ̂_k = n_k/n` for categorical data (Lagrange multiplier with `Σθ_k=1` constraint)
- Derive `μ̂ = (1/N)Σxᵢ` for Gaussian data (set `dℓ/dμ = 0`)

## Interview Q&A
??? question "MLE vs MAP — relationship?"
    MLE maximizes P(D|θ). MAP maximizes P(θ|D) ∝ P(D|θ)·P(θ), adding a prior that acts like regularization. MAP → MLE as data grows or the prior becomes uniform.

??? question "Why maximize log-likelihood instead of likelihood?"
    Products of many small probabilities underflow; log turns the product into a sum (`Σ log p`), which is numerically stable and easier to differentiate. argmax is unchanged since log is monotonic.
